TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua aya ta maretsekeɫ ti Tjukutjuku kati Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan; sa puaɫak tjai Muakakai tu nguanguaq aravats.
S.PHON: izua aja ta marəʦəkəʎ ti cukucuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan; sa puaʎak cai muakakai tu ŋuaŋuaq aravaʦ.
S.FORM: izua aya ta marecekelj ti Tjukutjuku kati Puljaljuljaljuyan; sa pualjak tjai Muakakai tu nguanguaq aravac.
S.PHON: izua aja ta marəʦəkəʎ ti ʦukuʦuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan; sa puaʎak ʦai muakakai tu ŋuaŋuaq aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: They say there was a married couple called Tjukutjuku and Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan. They had a very beautiful child called Muakakai.
The young men of the village fell in love with her;
and she married Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu, they say.
But they say her vagina had teeth.
They slept together,

S.FORM: manu katsen na kutji, sa patsay ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: manu kaʦən na kuci, sa paʦaj ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: manu kacen na kutji, sa pacay ti Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: manu kaʦən na kuʦi, sa paʦaj ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: and he was bitten by her vagina, and so Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu died.

S.FORM: saka maɫianga, sa "pai, gatsaɫu, kenamau anga," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka maʎiaŋa, sa "pai, gaʦaʎu, kənamau aŋa," aja ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: saka maljianga, sa "pai, gacalju, kenamau anga," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka maʎiaŋa, sa "pai, gaʦaʎu, kənamau aŋa," aja ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: In the morning Tjukutjuku said: “Get up and have breakfast.”
But he was dead.

S.FORM: saka "aku matsay? su kinuda?" aya kivadaq a matjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: saka "aku maʦajʔ su kinuda" aja kivadaq a macaʎaʎak.
S.FORM: saka "aku macay' su kinuda?" aya kivadaq a matjaljaljak.
S.PHON: saka "aku maʦajʔ su kinuda" aja kivadaq a maʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: “Why did he die? What did you do?” her parents asked.
Muakakai didn’t reply.

S.FORM: sa qaqivuin a sikataqaɫan ni Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu; saka mangtjez a malap.
S.PHON: sa qaqivuin a sikataqaʎan ni kuʎəʎuʎəʎu; saka maŋcəz a maɭap.
S.FORM: sa qaqivuin a sikataqaljan ni Kuljeljuljelju; saka mangtjez a malap.
S.PHON: sa qaqivuin a sikataqaʎan ni kuʎəʎuʎəʎu; saka maŋʦəz a maɭap.
S.TRANSL: They told Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu’s people, and they came and took his body.

S.FORM: saka "masiaq itjen," aya ti Tjukutjuku kati Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: saka "masiaq icən," aja ti cukucuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: saka "masiaq itjen," aya ti Tjukutjuku kati Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: saka "masiaq iʦən," aja ti ʦukuʦuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: “We’re ashamed of her,” said Tjukutjuku and Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: saka "sikudanga a aɫak; tja ɫangianaw," aya, sa piɫangian; saka paqaɫudj ta zaɫum pasa pana.
S.PHON: saka "sikudaŋa a aʎak; ca ʎaŋianaw," aja, sa piʎaŋian; saka paqaʎuɉ ta zaʎum pasa pana.
S.FORM: saka "sikudanga a aljak; tja ljangianaw," aya, sa piljangian; saka paqaljudj ta zaljum pasa pana.
S.PHON: saka "sikudaŋa a aʎak; ʦa ʎaŋianaw," aja, sa piʎaŋian; saka paqaʎuɉ ta zaʎum pasa pana.
S.TRANSL: “What use is this child? We’ll put her in a trunk,” they said, and put her in a trunk, and let it float away on the water of the river.

S.FORM: saka izua se tjarivavaw ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu katua sikataqaɫan tjepanapana.
S.PHON: saka izua sə carivavaw ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katua sikataqaʎan cəpanapana.
S.FORM: saka izua se tjarivavaw ti Kuljeljuljelju katua sikataqaljan tjepanapana.
S.PHON: saka izua sə ʦarivavaw ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katua sikataqaʎan ʦəpanapana.
S.TRANSL: Now a person from heaven called Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu was fishing in the river with his fellow-villagers.

S.FORM: sa djemumak anga ta ɫangian i taladj ta vatsaɫ.
S.PHON: sa ɉəmumak aŋa ta ʎaŋian i taɭaɉ ta vaʦaʎ.
S.FORM: sa djemumak anga ta ljangian i taladj ta vacalj.
S.PHON: sa ɉəmumak aŋa ta ʎaŋian i taɭaɉ ta vaʦaʎ.
S.TRANSL: They found the trunk in a deep pool.

S.FORM: sa alapen saka qaluin a sema umaq; saka mangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: sa aɭapən saka qaɭuin a səma umaq; saka maŋcəz i umaq.
S.FORM: sa alapen saka qaluin a sema umaq; saka mangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: sa aɭapən saka qaɭuin a səma umaq; saka maŋʦəz i umaq.
S.TRANSL: They took it and carried it home.
When they got home, he said: “Mother, father, we found a trunk.

S.FORM: aki anema i taladj? sadjelung aravats."
S.PHON: aki anəma i taɭaɉʔ saɉəɭuŋ aravaʦ."
S.FORM: aki anema i taladj' sadjelung aravac."
S.PHON: aki anəma i taɭaɉʔ saɉəɭuŋ aravaʦ."
S.TRANSL: What can be inside it? It’s very heavy.”
So they lifted the lid and looked inside.
“This must be Muakakai.

S.FORM: makeɫang aza puaɫis a kutji; siniverits na matjaɫaɫak, a siayaya."
S.PHON: makəʎaŋ aza puaʎis a kuci; sinivəriʦ na macaʎaʎak, a siajaja."
S.FORM: makeljang aza pualjis a kutji; siniveric na matjaljaljak, a siayaya."
S.PHON: makəʎaŋ aza puaʎis a kuʦi; sinivəriʦ na maʦaʎaʎak, a siajaja."
S.TRANSL: She is said to be well-known for her vagina with teeth, and for being thrown away by her parents.”
“Go off and hunt,” they said to Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
Muakakai went to sleep.

S.FORM: saka qetsapen aza aɫis na kutji.
S.PHON: saka qəʦapən aza aʎis na kuci.
S.FORM: saka qecapen aza aljis na kutji.
S.PHON: saka qəʦapən aza aʎis na kuʦi.
S.TRANSL: And they pulled out the teeth from her vagina.

S.FORM: saka mangtjez ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu a naqemaɫup.
S.PHON: saka maŋcəz ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a naqəmaʎup.
S.FORM: saka mangtjez ti Kuljeljuljelju a naqemaljup.
S.PHON: saka maŋʦəz ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a naqəmaʎup.
S.TRANSL: Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu came back from hunting.

S.FORM: saka masalud; saka inikanga matsay ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: saka masaɭud; saka inikaŋa maʦaj ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: saka masalud; saka inikanga macay ti Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: saka masaɭud; saka inikaŋa maʦaj ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: They slept together, and Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu didn’t die.
So Muakakai became pregnant, and they had a child.
The teeth were called tatalaven ḍaḍamelamen.
They were valuable enough to be exchanged for land.

S.FORM: sepatj a aɫis na kutji a qata.
S.PHON: səpac a aʎis na kuci a qata.
S.FORM: sepatj a aljis na kutji a qata.
S.PHON: səpaʦ a aʎis na kuʦi a qata.
S.TRANSL: There are four vagina teeth beads;
one at Payuan,
two at Peɫeti,
and one at Kaviangan.
The end.
